rationale.Existence & Specificity: There is no public commitment related to land use, marine ecosystems, or sustainable sourcing impacts.
rationale.Ambition: There is no defined ambition beyond regulatory compliance.
rationale.Coverage: There is no scope defined.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no disclosure of sourcing footprint or ecosystem impact.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on land or marine impact.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The evidence indicates that there are no public air pollutant reduction commitments disclosed.
rationale.Ambition: There is no defined ambition beyond regulatory compliance as no commitments are disclosed.
rationale.Coverage: The evidence does not define any scope for the company's air quality initiatives.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: No pollutant data is disclosed, indicating a lack of transparency.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on the company's air pollutant performance.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has clear, time-bound commitments for deforestation-free supply chains and regenerative agriculture by 2025.
rationale.Ambition: Targets of 100% deforestation-free supply and 30% regenerative agriculture are ambitious and in line with industry norms.
rationale.Coverage: The commitments cover key commodities globally; however, asset and value chain coverage specifics are vague for some commitments.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is a lack of specified methodologies, standards, and framework alignments, impacting transparency.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Regular progress is reported, with significant progress towards deforestation-free and regenerative agriculture targets.

Description.description: Danone commits to achieving a deforestation-free supply chain, targeting 100% compliance by 2025, from a 0% baseline in 2020.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers key commodities on a global scale within the deforestation aspect of the value chain.

Description.description: Danone has committed to transition 30% of key ingredients to regenerative agriculture by 2025, with a baseline set in 2020.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers global agricultural sourcing, specifically key ingredients sourced directly from farms transitioning to regenerative practices.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: No evidence or public commitment regarding hazardous chemicals was found.
rationale.Ambition: No ambition beyond regulatory compliance is indicated due to lack of evidence.
rationale.Coverage: There is no defined scope as there is no evidence provided.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: No public ingredient or chemical disclosure information is available.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on chemical phase-out progress as no evidence is provided.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: There is a clear quantified target of 100% reduction by 2030 with a baseline of 2020.
rationale.Ambition: While the target is 100% reduction by 2030, interim targets are not specified, partially limiting ambition clarity.
rationale.Coverage: The commitment covers global production sites using a 4R approach but lacks categorical breakdowns.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: The 4R methodology is mentioned but lacks validation or alignment with recognized frameworks.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress to 99.3% by 2024 shows measurable reductions with on-track status for 2030.

Description.description: Danone is committed to achieving 100% water stewardship and management across its production sites by 2030, with a baseline year of 2020.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers global production sites using the 4R approach, encompassing total asset coverage in water management.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a fully defined net-zero target for 2050 with a 2030 milestone covering its entire value chain.
rationale.Ambition: There is a 1.5°C-aligned ambition across scopes with clear near-term reductions, but without specific breakdowns in immediate timeframes.
rationale.Coverage: The commitments explicitly include Scope 1, 2, and 3 with global coverage and full value chain inclusion.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: The targets are aligned with SBTi 1.5°C, but lack explicit third-party validation details.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is consistent reporting showing measurable reductions against milestones, yet some data indicates negative progress.

Description.description: Danone has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2050, with interim targets for a 16.1% reduction by 2030 compared to a 2020 baseline, covering the entire value chain on an absolute basis.
Description.coverage: The commitment encompasses Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions globally, covering the full value chain.
Description.validation: The commitment is aligned with the SBTi 1.5°C methodology, but external validation status is not specified.

Description.description: Danone commits to reducing its Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 36% by 2025, using 2020 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all global operations and includes total asset emissions within Scope 1 and 2.

Description.description: Danone commits to achieving Net Zero Scope 3 GHG emissions by 2050, with a 16.1% reduction as an interim target by 2030, using a 2020 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers the entire global value chain under Scope 3 emissions.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has quantified targets with baseline years and target years for all commitments provided.
rationale.Ambition: Ambitious targets are set with precise percentages and timelines but fall slightly short of the highest industry norms.
rationale.Coverage: The commitments cover global scope and entire asset coverage, but only for the specified packaging categories.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is a lack of specified methodologies and standards, with only minimal external alignment mentioned.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is disclosed and on track for some targets, yet off track for others, indicating mixed performance.

Description.description: Danone is committed to achieving a 100% circular economy/recycling target for plastic by 2040, with an interim goal of 60% by 2024, measured against a 2020 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers plastic globally, encompassing the entire value chain including recycling and recovery.

Description.description: Danone is committed to making 100% of its packaging recyclable, reusable, or compostable by 2030, with an interim target of 85% by 2024.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all packaging globally, addressing the entire value chain through reusability, recyclability, and compostability.

Description.description: Danone aims to reduce virgin fossil-based plastic packaging by 30% by 2030 and 50% by 2040, with an interim target of 8% reduction by 2024 from a 2020 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Danone's global operations, focusing on virgin fossil-based packaging materials across its entire value chain.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: A 15% intensity reduction target by 2025 is specified with a baseline year of 2019.
rationale.Ambition: The 15% reduction target for intensity by 2025 is moderately ambitious.
rationale.Coverage: The target covers manufacturing facilities globally, focusing on direct operations.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is limited disclosure and no evidence of external validation.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is reported as off-track with current progress at -8.10% compared to the baseline.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz has committed to a 15% reduction in freshwater withdrawal intensity by 2025, using 2019 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all manufacturing facilities globally.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a specific and quantified target of net zero by 2050 and a 50% reduction by 2030, with a clear baseline year of 2021.
rationale.Ambition: The company's targets are moderately ambitious with absolute reduction goals of 50% by 2030, which is a meaningful reduction.
rationale.Coverage: The commitments cover Scope 1, 2, and material Scope 3 with a global value chain inclusion.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no information regarding methodology or validation, leading to low credibility.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting of progress against the baseline, resulting in no evidence of delivery.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2050, with an interim target of reducing emissions by 50% by 2030, using 2021 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions across the entire global value chain.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz has committed to a 50% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 2030, using 2021 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment applies globally across Scope 1 and 2 emissions within the company's operations.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz aims to reduce its Scope 3 greenhouse gas emissions by 50% by 2030, using 2021 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers global operations across the full value chain in Scope 3 emissions.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a clear, quantified, and time-bound commitment of 100% deforestation-free supply chain by 2025.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition is meaningful with specific timelines and numeric values for 100% achievement by 2025, aligning with industry norms.
rationale.Coverage: The commitment covers primary deforestation-linked commodities including palm oil, sugar, beef, dairy, cocoa, coffee, pulp, paper, and packaging.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: While there is methodology reference, alignment with recognized frameworks like TNFD is not explicitly mentioned.
rationale.Delivery/Status: The company reports a 64% progress against the baseline but lacks detailed year-on-year transparency.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz aims for a 100% deforestation-free supply chain for primary deforestation-linked commodities by 2025, with a baseline year of 2019.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers key commodities such as palm oil, sugar, beef, dairy, cocoa, coffee, pulp, paper, and packaging.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Targets are quantified with specific percentages and clearly defined timeframes for both virgin plastic reduction and recyclable packaging.
rationale.Ambition: Targets reflect meaningful ambition at 20% virgin plastic reduction by 2030 and 100% recyclability by 2025, which aligns with industry norms.
rationale.Coverage: Commitments cover the global portfolio, addressing major aspects like virgin plastic and packaging comprehensively.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Lacks baseline packaging footprint disclosure and reporting references to recognized standards.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is disclosed for recyclable packaging but not for virgin plastic reduction or other targets.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz has committed to making 100% of its packaging recyclable, reusable, or compostable by 2025, with a baseline year of 2019.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all packaging globally within its value chain.

Description.description: Kraft-Heinz has committed to reducing virgin plastic use by 20% by 2030, using a 2021 baseline.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers its global portfolio and includes the entire value chain related to virgin plastic.

rationale.Coverage: The focus is on primary supply chains, covering key deforestation-linked commodities globally.

Description.description: Nestle aims to achieve a deforestation-free supply chain with a target of 100% by 2025, covering raw materials like meat, palm oil, pulp and paper, soy, sugar, cocoa, and coffee, with a baseline year of 2018.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers the primary supply chains on a global scale, but the specific asset coverage is not disclosed.

Description.description: Nestlé aims to achieve 20% regenerative agriculture by 2025 and 50% by 2030, using 2018 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers key ingredients globally, but specific assets and the value chain are not included.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a fully defined net-zero target for 2050 with clear 2030 milestones covering all scopes.
rationale.Ambition: The targets align with 1.5°C scenarios, but lack substantial detail on interim reductions and numeric specifics.
rationale.Coverage: Scopes 1, 2, and 3 are included with comprehensive global coverage across all activities.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Targets are approved by the SBTi, but there is a lack of detailed baseline recalculation methodology.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Interim progress is reported as on track, but there is a lack of specific milestone reporting and detailed progress data.

Description.description: Nestle commits to achieving a 100% reduction in net zero emissions by 2050, using 2018 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all scopes (1, 2, and 3) globally, including all activities throughout the value chain.

Description.description: Nestle aims to reduce its combined Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 50% by 2030 and 90% by 2050, based on a 2018 baseline.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all scopes globally across Nestle's energy and industry assets, including the entire value chain.
Description.validation: The commitment is externally validated and approved by the Science Based Targets initiative.

Description.description: The commitment is to reduce Scope 3 GHG emissions by 50% by 2030 and 75% by 2050, using a 2018 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers global operations, specifically focusing on forests, land, and agriculture within the Scope 3 value chain.
Description.validation: The commitment is validated by the Science Based Targets initiative and aligned with the Net Zero Roadmap.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The target is to achieve 100% AWS certification by 2025 but lacks baseline year and specific value details.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition is to achieve 100% certification by 2025, which is meaningful, but there are no interim or long-term targets.
rationale.Coverage: Covers all Nestlé Waters sites globally, although there are no specifics on value chain inclusion beyond certification.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: The methodology aligns with the Alliance for Water Stewardship framework, but there is no validation status.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Current progress is 39.8% as of 2024, with trajectory off track and missing intermediate milestones.

Description.description: The commitment is to achieve 100% AWS certification for Nestlé Waters sites by 2025.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers all Nestlé Waters sites globally, focusing on site certification.
Description.validation: The commitment is aligned with the AWS certification, but external validation status is not specified.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Multiple quantified targets with clear definitions and timelines for virgin plastic and recyclable packaging are provided, although some details are missing.
rationale.Ambition: The targets are ambitious with high percentages for virgin plastic reduction and recyclability, but lack interim and long-term targets.
rationale.Coverage: Global geographical coverage is stated, but specific details on asset and value chain coverage are not provided.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Disclosure lacks methodology, standards, and validation details, reducing transparency despite some framework alignment.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress reports are provided with percentage achievements, but the trajectory is off track for virgin plastic reduction.

Description.description: Pepsi commits to achieving 100% net zero emissions across Scope 1, 2, and 3 by 2050, using 2022 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions with unspecified geographic coverage.

Description.description: Pepsi aims to achieve a 50% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 2030, using 2022 as the baseline year.

Description.description: Pepsi has committed to reducing its Scope 3 GHG emissions by 42% in Energy & Industry and 30% in Forest, Land, and Agriculture by 2030, using 2022 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers total asset Scope 3 emissions across Energy & Industry and FLAG categories, with geographic coverage not specified.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a clear quantified target with baseline and target year specified.
rationale.Ambition: The company has a meaningful target for 2025 but lacks interim and longer-term targets.
rationale.Coverage: The commitment covers high water-risk facilities but lacks comprehensive geographic and asset coverage.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is a use of the AWS Standard without detailed framework alignment or validation status.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is measurable progress with 86% achieved and a trajectory described as 'On Track'.

Description.description: Pepsi aims for 100% water replenishment in high water-risk manufacturing facilities by 2025, starting from a 2022 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers company-owned high water-risk facilities, but geographic specifics are not disclosed.
Description.validation: The commitment follows the AWS Standard, but external validation is not specified.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has defined quantified targets with baselines and target years for primary and secondary packaging.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition of reducing virgin plastic and increasing recyclable packaging is moderately aligned with industry norms, but lacks interim targets.
rationale.Coverage: The commitment covers primary and secondary packaging in key markets but lacks detail on asset and value chain coverage.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no evidence of methodology, validation, or framework alignment provided.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is measurable with an achieved trajectory, notably 5% reduction in virgin plastic and 93% recyclable packaging.

Description.description: Pepsi has committed to achieving 97% recyclable, reusable, or compostable packaging by 2030, using a 2022 baseline of 0%.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers primary and secondary packaging in key packaging markets.

Description.description: Pepsi aims to reduce virgin plastic in primary plastic packaging by 2% year-over-year through 2030, using 2022 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers primary plastic packaging in key packaging markets; asset coverage and value chain inclusion are not specified.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: There are clear, time-bound commitments with quantified targets for some activities.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition aligns with industry norms, particularly with absolute targets for 2025 and 2030.
rationale.Coverage: Coverage includes high-risk commodities but lacks geographic specificity and value chain inclusion details.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: The policy refers to a stewardship framework, but lacks reporting on validation or framework alignment.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is reported above 90% for deforestation-free targets and ~66% for regenerative agriculture, but is marked as off-track in some areas.

Description.description: Pepsi aims for a 100% deforestation-free supply chain for high-risk commodities by 2025, using 2022 as a baseline.

Description.description: Unilever has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2039, with baseline year set at 2021.
Description.coverage: Not disclosed.

Description.description: Unilever commits to reducing its Scope 3 GHG emissions by 42% by 2030, using a baseline year of 2021.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers energy and industrial emissions within its Scope 3 category, encompassing the entire value chain.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a clear quantified target for reaching 100 locations in water-stressed areas by 2030 with a specified baseline year.
rationale.Ambition: The targets are meaningful and include specific percentages, timelines, and both interim and long-term goals.
rationale.Coverage: The programs cover water-stressed areas but lack detail on scope related to all owned manufacturing sites.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is some mention of methodology or framework alignment, but insufficient detail is provided.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is year-on-year reporting with a 21% progress reported towards the 2030 target.

Description.description: Unilever aims to implement water stewardship programs in 100 locations in water-stressed areas by 2030, with a baseline starting at zero in 2021.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers water-stressed areas specifically and focuses on implementing programs within these regions.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Multiple quantified targets with clear definitions but missing time boundaries for the deforestation-free target.
rationale.Ambition: Targets show alignment with global frameworks but lack comprehensive coverage with exact percentages across all commodities.
rationale.Coverage: Covers primary deforestation-linked commodities and agricultural land but appears limited in full global scope representation.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Some methodological details and validation are mentioned but lack regular, transparent updates for all targets.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Demonstrates a multi-year reduction in exposure, especially with the habitat protection target achieved, but not all targets have current reporting.

Description.description: The commitment is to achieve a 97% deforestation-free supply chain for primary deforestation-linked commodities, with ongoing targets and a baseline year of 2021.

Description.description: Unilever commits to protecting and regenerating 1.5 million hectares of land, forests, and oceans by 2027, starting from a baseline of 0 hectares in 2021.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Unilever programmes with specific scope definitions including geographic and categorical breakdowns.

Description.description: Unilever commits to implementing regenerative agriculture on 1 million hectares by 2030, with a baseline year of 2021 starting at 0 hectares.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers agricultural land with specific geographic and asset coverage details not disclosed.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Multiple quantified targets exist with clear definitions and timelines, including a 100% recyclability and various virgin plastic reduction targets.
rationale.Ambition: While targets are specific with timelines, they reflect industry norms rather than exceeding them significantly.
rationale.Coverage: The commitments cover plastic packaging in 26 countries, but do not include all brands or formats globally.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Quantitative targets and progress are disclosed, but external assurance and detailed methodology documentation are lacking.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is reported with some targets on track, but others are off-track or lack reporting, showing inconsistent delivery against targets.

Description.description: Unilever commits to collect and process more plastic packaging than it sells by 2025, with a baseline year of 2019.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers plastic packaging in 26 countries.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: A quantified water reduction target exists, specifying a 50% reduction by 2030 with a defined baseline year of 2015.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition aligns with sector norms, targeting a 50% reduction by 2030 in absolute terms.
rationale.Coverage: Covers part of the operations, specifically 19 target sites within water-scarce areas, lacking full asset coverage.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Limited disclosure; no alignment with recognized standards or detailed methodology is provided.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is a 27% reduction from baseline, but the trajectory is off track, showing some measurable progress.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has quantified targets but lacks defined baseline and interim targets for some commitments.
rationale.Ambition: The targets are absolute with clear percentages and timelines, demonstrating moderate ambition.
rationale.Coverage: Most targets cover Scope 1, 2, or 3 emissions fully but lack detailed value chain inclusion.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Baseline for most targets is mentioned, but there is limited methodological clarity and external validation.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Some progress data is disclosed but tracking against the target is limited and off-track.

Description.description: Anglo-American has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2040, based on a baseline year of 2020 with absolute targets.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Scope 1 and 2 emissions globally for all assets, but does not provide specific details on the value chain.
Description.validation: This commitment is third-party verified and aligned with a 1.5°C trajectory.

Description.description: The commitment is to achieve a 30% absolute reduction in Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030, using 2016 as the baseline year with a baseline value of 13.41 Mt CO2e.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all geographic and asset areas under the company's operations, but does not include details about the value chain.

Description.description: Anglo-American commits to a 50% reduction in Scope 3 GHG emissions by 2040 from a 2020 baseline of 165.1 Mt CO2e.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all geographic areas and assets but does not detail the value chain inclusion.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Minimal specificity is provided for tailings management conformance, but most commitments lack any detail.
rationale.Ambition: There is a partial demonstration of ambition with a 100% conformance target, yet no ambition beyond regulatory compliance is evident.
rationale.Coverage: Coverage is partially addressed with the inclusion of the tailings facilities, but scope is limited.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is reference to frameworks such as GISTM, but there is no disclosure of actual waste data.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on progress or status for any commitments.

Description.description: Anglo-American commits to achieving 100% conformance in tailings management by the year 2025.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers tailings facilities in total but only applies to lower consequence categories, with no specific geographic limit disclosed.
Description.validation: The commitment is aligned with the GISTM and ICMM frameworks, but it is not externally validated.

Description.description: Anglo-American has set a timebound target to achieve a net positive impact on habitat protection and regeneration by 2030, with the baseline year being 2018.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers biodiversity initiatives within their managed operations, but details regarding asset and value chain inclusion are not specified.
Description.validation: The commitment uses the mitigation hierarchy and aligns with biodiversity stewardship frameworks, but external validation is not detailed.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Commitment not disclosed in provided data, indicating no public commitment.
rationale.Ambition: No ambition beyond regulatory compliance, as no commitments are disclosed.
rationale.Coverage: No scope is defined as commitments are not disclosed.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: No disclosure of waste volumes or tailings data is evidenced.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on waste or material efficiency performance.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a quantified water efficiency target with a defined baseline year and target year, albeit with limited scope.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition includes a meaningful percentage target and timelines, but lacks clarity on alignment with sector norms.
rationale.Coverage: The coverage is limited to two mining operations in Chile, with no mention of global operations or water-stressed regions.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is a lack of alignment with recognized standards and minimal disclosure on methodology or validation.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Progress is reported with a trajectory mentioned, but lacks year-on-year detail for all operations.

Description.description: BHP commits to a 10% improvement in water efficiency in its mining operations by 2030, using 2022 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers mining operations specifically at Escondida and Pampa Norte in Chile.
Description.validation: The commitment is not externally validated, certified, or assured.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a quantified absolute reduction target of 30% by 2030 with a clear baseline year of 2020.
rationale.Ambition: The targets set (30% by 2030) align with sector pathways but are not the most ambitious possible.
rationale.Coverage: Scope 1 and 2 GHG are fully covered, but Scope 3 is only partially addressed, focusing on specific operations.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no mention of alignment with recognized standards like the GHG Protocol.
rationale.Delivery/Status: A 36% reduction has already been achieved, exceeding the 2030 target, demonstrating strong delivery.

Description.description: BHP has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2050, targeting a 100% reduction in absolute emissions from a 2020 baseline of 13.6 Mt.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers global Scope 1 and 2 emissions from operated assets.

Description.description: BHP has committed to a 30% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2030, using 2020 as the baseline year with an initial value of 13.6 million tonnes.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers BHP's operated assets globally and does not include the value chain.

Description.description: BHP commits to achieving net zero Scope 3 GHG emissions by 2050, using 2020's 352 MtCO2-e as a baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all global operations and involves customers in steelmaking.

Description.description: The commitment is to conserve 30% of areas under conservation by 2030, with a baseline year of 2020 and an absolute target magnitude.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers areas of highest ecosystem value, specifically focusing on land and water stewarded, without value chain inclusion.
Description.validation: The commitment's validation status is not disclosed.

Description.description: BHP's commitment involves respecting protected areas with an ongoing and non-timebound approach, without specific quantified targets.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers BHP's operated assets globally, without inclusion of the broader value chain.

Description.description: BHP commits to reducing NOx emissions by 47% by 2030, using 2005 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers NOx emissions from BHP's global cement manufacturing operations.

Description.description: The company commits to a 67% reduction in SOx emissions intensity by 2030, using 2005 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers SOx emissions from global cement manufacturing operations, excluding the value chain.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: There is no public water-related commitment, only unreported intentions.
rationale.Ambition: The company shows no ambition beyond compliance with permits.
rationale.Coverage: There is no defined scope for their water-related activities.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no disclosure of water withdrawal, consumption, or discharge data.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on water performance.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: No public commitment regarding waste rock or material efficiency exists; only regulatory compliance statements are mentioned.
rationale.Ambition: The evidence indicates no ambition beyond regulatory compliance in terms of waste management or material efficiency.
rationale.Coverage: No scope is defined within the provided evidence.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no disclosure of waste volumes or tailings data present in the evidence.
rationale.Delivery/Status: The company provides no reporting on waste or material efficiency performance.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has quantified targets with a baseline year and multiple target years, including interim milestones.
rationale.Ambition: The targets indicate meaningful reductions with specific percentages and timelines, aligned with long-term and interim goals.
rationale.Coverage: Scope 1, 2, and 3 are mentioned, but geographic coverage and detailed value chain inclusion are not specified.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Lack of detail in methodology, validation status, or alignment with frameworks reduces transparency.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is observable progress against the baseline with a reported reduction, but milestone details are lacking.

Description.description: Glencore commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2050, using a 2019 baseline.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers combined Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions with industrial asset focus; geographic and value chain specifics are not disclosed.

Description.description: Glencore aims to reduce Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 15% by 2026, 25% by 2030, and 50% by 2035, using 2019 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions from industrial assets, but does not specify geographic or value chain inclusion.

Description.description: Glencore commits to reducing Scope 3 GHG emissions by 15% by 2026, 25% by 2030, and 50% by 2035 from a 2019 baseline of 546.5 million tonnes of CO2 equivalent.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers combined Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions related to Glencore's industrial operations, with specific geographic and value chain coverage not disclosed.

Description.description: Glencore has committed to respecting protected areas by ensuring no exploration or mining occurs in UNESCO World Heritage and IUCN I-IV areas, with a target year of 2024.
Description.coverage: This commitment covers UNESCO World Heritage areas and IUCN I-IV globally, but does not specify asset or value chain coverage.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The evidence shows no public water-related commitment; all details are not disclosed.
rationale.Ambition: There is no evidence of ambition beyond compliance as no commitments are disclosed.
rationale.Coverage: No scope is defined since all commitments are undisclosed.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no disclosure of water withdrawal, consumption, or discharge data.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on water performance provided.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has quantified absolute targets with defined baseline and target years, including interim milestones.
rationale.Ambition: Targets are aligned with sector pathways, with interim and long-term goals showing meaningful reduction ambitions.
rationale.Coverage: Scope 1 and 2 emissions are fully covered, with specific Scope 3 categories identified.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Reporting is aligned with recognized standards and frameworks, but some details on recalculation are unclear.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Annual reporting shows progress but indicates off-track status with significant gaps in current achievements.

Description.description: Rio-Tinto has committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2050, with interim targets of a 15% reduction by 2025 and a 50% reduction by 2030, based on a 2018 baseline of 35.7 Mt CO2e.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Scope 1 and 2 emissions globally within its assets and operations.
Description.validation: The commitment aligns with 1.5°C pathways but is not stated to be externally validated.

Description.description: Rio-Tinto aims to reduce its Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 50% by 2030, using a 2018 baseline of 35.7 Mt CO2e.
Description.coverage: The target covers both Scope 1 and 2 emissions but does not specify geographic or asset-specific coverage.
Description.validation: The commitment is aligned with 1.5°C pathways, but external validation or certification is not disclosed.

Description.description: Rio-Tinto commits to a 50% reduction in Scope 3 GHG emissions by 2035, using 2022 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment specifically covers IOC high-grade ores within the Scope 3 emissions category, but geographic and asset coverage are not disclosed.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: Evidence of a quantified target exists only for tailings management, limited to specific facilities.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition is demonstrated only for tailings management, with no further ambition beyond compliance evident.
rationale.Coverage: The scope is limited to facilities with 'Very High' or 'Extreme' consequence classification in tailings management.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: Credibility is tied to GISTM alignment, but broader waste volume disclosure is lacking.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is progress reporting only on tailings management for specific facilities.

Description.description: Rio-Tinto aims for 100% conformance with the Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management (GISTM) by 2025, with baseline set in 2018.
Description.coverage: The commitment specifically targets facilities classified as having 'Very High' or 'Extreme' consequences.

Description.description: Rio-Tinto aims to halt and reverse biodiversity loss by 2030, with a full recovery targeted by 2050.
Description.coverage: This commitment has a global scope as part of a nature-positive goal.

Description.description: The commitment involves respecting legally designated protected areas without a quantified target or specific timeline.
Description.coverage: The commitment specifically covers legally designated protected areas within the company's mining operations.

Description.description: The commitment is to reduce NOx emissions with a baseline year of 2018, but it does not provide a quantified target or timeframe.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers the Iron Ore Company of Canada.

Description.description: The commitment aims to reduce SOx emissions with a baseline year of 2018, but it lacks a quantified target, defined magnitude, or set timeline.
Description.coverage: The commitment specifically covers the Iron Ore Company of Canada.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The evidence shows no disclosed commitments and only regulatory compliance statements.
rationale.Ambition: There is no indication of ambitions beyond compliance with permits.
rationale.Coverage: No scope is defined as per the provided evidence.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no disclosure of water withdrawal, consumption, or discharge data.
rationale.Delivery/Status: There is no reporting on water performance.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: There is minimal evidence of a public commitment to waste rock or tailings reduction, with only compliance statements present.
rationale.Ambition: The ambition is vaguely indicated, focusing on a zero industrial waste disposal target without clear evidence beyond regulatory compliance.
rationale.Coverage: The scope of the commitments is poorly defined with minimal details on geographic or asset coverage.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no transparency regarding waste volumes or tailings data, with methodology and validation details missing.
rationale.Delivery/Status: No progress is reported on the commitments, lacking data on current status or achievements.

Description.description: The commitment aims for zero industrial waste disposal by the year 2040, using 2020 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: Not disclosed.

rationale.Existence & Specificity: The company has a specified net-zero target for 2050 with a 2030 milestone.
rationale.Ambition: The company has defined specific targets and timelines, but alignment to 1.5°C is not explicit.
rationale.Coverage: Scope 1, 2, and 3 are addressed, but JV treatment and full global coverage are unclear.
rationale.Credibility & Transparency: There is no evidence of third-party target validation or transparency in method disclosure.
rationale.Delivery/Status: Some progress noted, but reporting is inconsistent across commitments.

Description.description: Teck commits to a 33% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions intensity by 2030, using 2020 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: Not disclosed.

Description.description: Teck has committed to achieving net-zero emissions by 2050 with a baseline year of 2020.
Description.coverage: Not disclosed.

Description.description: Teck commits to achieving net-zero Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2050, using 2020 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Scope 1 and 2 emissions across all geographical and asset areas relevant to Teck's operations.

Description.description: Teck is committed to achieving net-zero Scope 3 GHG emissions by 2050, using 2020 as the baseline year.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers Scope 3 emissions with geographic, asset, and value chain inclusion not disclosed.

Description.description: Teck commits to a target of conserving or rehabilitating 3 hectares for every hectare disturbed, with a target year of 2030 and a baseline of 2020.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers all mining activities within the company.

Description.description: Not disclosed.
Description.coverage: The commitment covers exploration and mining activities.

Themes.Practice: Unilever commits to a 95% reduction of deforestation-linked commodities and the protection of 1 million hectares of agricultural land by 2030, with progress aligned to independently validated frameworks.
Danone targets net zero emissions for its entire value chain by 2050, with interim targets and a 16.1% reduction by 2030, aligning with SBTi 1.5°C and disclosed in its Climate Transition Plan.

Unilever targets reducing plastic packaging by 30% by 2026 and 40% by 2028, achieving 100% recyclable rigids by 2030, and committing to collect and process more plastic than they sell by 2025, with scope covering 26 countries and alignment validated by internal frameworks.
Danone targets a 100% implementation of the 4R water stewardship approach across global production sites by 2030, starting from a 2020 baseline, with progress disclosed as 99.3% by 2024.
Teck targets net-zero GHG emissions across Scope 1, 2, and 3 by 2050, with a 33% intensity reduction by 2030 from a 2020 baseline, using a validated framework aligned with SBTi.
Anglo-American targets 100% conformance with lower consequence tailings facilities by 2025, aligning with ICMM and using the GISTM framework.
Anglo-American targets a 50% reduction in fresh water abstraction by 2030 from a 2015 baseline of 48,666 ML in water scarce areas, with current status and validation framework not disclosed.

action_items.description: Align targets and methodologies with independently validated frameworks for transparency and credibility in biodiversity and ecological welfare commitments.
Develop and validate a methodology for demonstrating progress and ensure alignment with recognized ESG frameworks.
Extend commitments to include the protection and restoration of a specific magnitude of hectares of agricultural land by 2030, following independently validated frameworks.
Align GHG targets with SBTi's 1.5°C framework for increased credibility and transparency.
Commit to collect and process more plastic packaging than sold by 2025, with clear geographic and scope definitions similar to Unilever's framework.
Implement and disclose progress on the 4R water stewardship approach across all global production sites.
Align biodiversity and deforestation targets with independently validated frameworks to enhance credibility and transparency.
Establish and disclose interim GHG reduction targets for 2030, aligned with SBTi 1.5°C pathways, and publish these in a Climate Transition Plan.
Expand the current scope to include a commitment to collect and process more virgin plastic than the company sells by 2025, validated by a relevant methodology or standard.
Develop and disclose interim milestones for AWS certification progress to enhance transparency and track progress towards 100% target by 2025.
Align progress on biodiversity targets with an independently validated framework to enhance credibility and transparency.
Align GHG emissions reduction targets with the SBTi 1.5°C framework and disclose in a Climate Transition Plan.
Expand the scope to explicitly include methodologies and frameworks for validation and alignment with established standards, enhancing credibility and transparency.
Expand water stewardship target coverage to include global production sites, with comprehensive geographic scope beyond high water-risk facilities.
Submit GHG emissions targets to the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for alignment with a 1.5°C pathway and validation.
Expand water stewardship target to include 100% implementation across all global production sites by 2030, not limited to water-stressed areas.
Submit GHG emissions reduction targets to the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for validation to enhance credibility and transparency.
Submit GHG emissions reduction targets for validation with the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi).
Set a quantified target aligned with the Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management (GISTM) for tailings facility conformance by a specific year.
Expand water stewardship targets to include reductions in fresh water abstraction in water scarce areas, with a specific percentage reduction goal and baseline year for these areas.
Submit GHG emission targets to the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) for validation and public alignment with a recognized standard.
Set a target for 100% conformance with lower consequence tailings facilities by 2025 using the GISTM framework.
Establish and disclose a specific, quantified target for reducing fresh water abstraction in water scarce areas by 2030, including a baseline year and value.
Develop and publish a validated target for Scope 3 emissions aligned with SBTi guidelines to encompass the full value chain.
Expand targets to include facilities with 'Lower consequence' classifications for comprehensive risk management by 2025.
Set a quantified target for reduction in fresh water abstraction with a specific baseline year and value, focusing on water scarce areas, and establish a timeline for achievement.
Align material use, waste, and circularity targets with the GISTM framework and ICMM standards for increased credibility and transparency.
Develop and disclose a quantified target for water reduction in water scarce areas by 2030, including baseline year and baseline values.
